ref.pp: | and
| iff
| eq

docString:
Different propositional constructors that are variants of "and" for the purposes of the
theorem prover.

ref.pp:
/-- Different propositional constructors that are variants of "and" for the purposes of the
theorem prover. -/
inductive AndKind
  | and
  | iff
  | eq
  deriving Lean.ToExpr, DecidableEq

instance instToExprAndKind : ToExpr✝ (@Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.AndKind✝.{})
    where
  toExpr✝ := instToExprAndKind.toExpr
  toTypeExpr✝ := Expr.const✝ `Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.AndKind []

instance : DecidableEq✝ Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.AndKind✝ := fun x✝ y✝ =>
  if h✝ : x.ctorIdx✝ = y.ctorIdx✝ then
    isTrue✝
      (by
        first
        | have aux✝ := congrArg✝ Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.AndKind.ofNat h✝;
          rw [Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.AndKind.ofNat_ctorIdx, Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.AndKind.ofNat_ctorIdx] at aux✝;
          assumption
        | rfl)
  else isFalse✝ fun h✝ => by subst h✝; contradiction

value.pp:
:= fun x✝ y✝ =>
  if h✝ : x.ctorIdx✝ = y.ctorIdx✝ then
    isTrue✝
      (by
        first
        | have aux✝ := congrArg✝ Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.AndKind.ofNat h✝;
          rw [Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.AndKind.ofNat_ctorIdx, Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.AndKind.ofNat_ctorIdx] at aux✝;
          assumption
        | rfl)
  else isFalse✝ fun h✝ => by subst h✝; contradiction

ref.pp:
instance : Inhabited AndKind :=
  ⟨AndKind.and⟩
| var : Nat → IProp
| true : IProp
| false : IProp
| and' : AndKind → IProp → IProp → IProp
| or : IProp → IProp → IProp
| imp : IProp → IProp → IProp

docString: A reified inductive type for propositional logic.

ref.pp:
/-- A reified inductive type for propositional logic. -/
inductive IProp : Type
  | var : Nat → IProp
  | true : IProp
  | false : IProp
  | and' : AndKind → IProp → IProp → IProp
  | or : IProp → IProp → IProp
  | imp : IProp → IProp → IProp
  deriving Lean.ToExpr

instance instToExprIProp : ToExpr✝ (@Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.IProp✝.{})
    where
  toExpr✝ := instToExprIProp.toExpr
  toTypeExpr✝ := Expr.const✝ `Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.IProp []

docString: Constructor for `p ∧ q`.

ref.pp:
/-- Constructor for `p ∧ q`. -/
@[match_pattern, expose]
def and : IProp → IProp → IProp :=
  .and' .and

docString: Constructor for `p ↔ q`.

ref.pp:
/-- Constructor for `p ↔ q`. -/
@[match_pattern, expose]
def iff : IProp → IProp → IProp :=
  .and' .iff

docString: Constructor for `p = q`.

ref.pp:
/-- Constructor for `p = q`. -/
@[match_pattern, expose]
def eq : IProp → IProp → IProp :=
  .and' .eq

docString: Constructor for `¬ p`.

ref.pp:
/-- Constructor for `¬ p`. -/
@[match_pattern, expose]
def not (a : IProp) : IProp :=
  a.imp .false

docString: Constructor for `xor p q`.

ref.pp:
/-- Constructor for `xor p q`. -/
@[match_pattern, expose]
def xor (a b : IProp) : IProp :=
  (a.and b.not).or (b.and a.not)

instance : Inhabited IProp :=
  ⟨IProp.true⟩

docString: Given the contents of an `And` variant, return the two conjuncts.

ref.pp:
/-- Given the contents of an `And` variant, return the two conjuncts. -/
def sides : AndKind → IProp → IProp → IProp × IProp
  | .and, A, B => (A, B)
  | _, A, B => (A.imp B, B.imp A)

value.pp:
| .and, A, B => (A, B)
  | _, A, B => (A.imp B, B.imp A)

docString: Debugging printer for propositions.

ref.pp:
/-- Debugging printer for propositions. -/
def format : IProp → Std.Format
  | .var i => f! "v{i}"
  | .true => f!"⊤"
  | .false => f!"⊥"
  | .and p q => f! "({p.format } ∧ {q.format})"
  | .iff p q => f! "({p.format } ↔ {q.format})"
  | .eq p q => f! "({p.format } = {q.format})"
  | .or p q => f! "({p.format } ∨ {q.format})"
  | .imp p q => f! "({p.format } → {q.format})"

value.pp:
| .var i => f! "v{i}"
  | .true => f!"⊤"
  | .false => f!"⊥"
  | .and p q => f! "({p.format } ∧ {q.format})"
  | .iff p q => f! "({p.format } ↔ {q.format})"
  | .eq p q => f! "({p.format } = {q.format})"
  | .or p q => f! "({p.format } ∨ {q.format})"
  | .imp p q => f! "({p.format } → {q.format})"

ref.pp:
instance : Std.ToFormat IProp :=
  ⟨IProp.format⟩

docString: A comparator for `AndKind`. (There should really be a derive handler for this.)

ref.pp:
/-- A comparator for `AndKind`. (There should really be a derive handler for this.) -/
def cmp (p q : AndKind) : Ordering := by
  cases p <;> cases q
  exacts [.eq, .lt, .lt, .gt, .eq, .lt, .gt, .gt, .eq]

value.pp:
:= by
  cases p <;> cases q
  exacts [.eq, .lt, .lt, .gt, .eq, .lt, .gt, .gt, .eq]

docString: A comparator for propositions. (There should really be a derive handler for this.)

ref.pp:
/-- A comparator for propositions. (There should really be a derive handler for this.) -/
def cmp (p q : IProp) : Ordering := by
  cases p <;> cases q
  case var.var p q => exact compare p q
  case true.true => exact .eq
  case false.false => exact .eq
  case and'.and' ap p₁ p₂ aq q₁ q₂ => exact (ap.cmp aq).then <| (p₁.cmp q₁).then (p₂.cmp q₂)
  case or.or p₁ p₂ q₁ q₂ => exact (p₁.cmp q₁).then (p₂.cmp q₂)
  case imp.imp p₁ p₂ q₁ q₂ => exact (p₁.cmp q₁).then (p₂.cmp q₂)
  exacts [.lt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .gt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .gt, .gt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .gt, .gt, .gt, .lt, .lt, .gt, .gt,
    .gt, .gt, .lt, .gt, .gt, .gt, .gt, .gt]

value.pp:
:= by
  cases p <;> cases q
  case var.var p q => exact compare p q
  case true.true => exact .eq
  case false.false => exact .eq
  case and'.and' ap p₁ p₂ aq q₁ q₂ => exact (ap.cmp aq).then <| (p₁.cmp q₁).then (p₂.cmp q₂)
  case or.or p₁ p₂ q₁ q₂ => exact (p₁.cmp q₁).then (p₂.cmp q₂)
  case imp.imp p₁ p₂ q₁ q₂ => exact (p₁.cmp q₁).then (p₂.cmp q₂)
  exacts [.lt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .gt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .gt, .gt, .lt, .lt, .lt, .gt, .gt, .gt, .lt, .lt, .gt, .gt,
    .gt, .gt, .lt, .gt, .gt, .gt, .gt, .gt]

ref.pp:
instance : LT IProp :=
  ⟨fun p q => p.cmp q = .lt⟩
instance : DecidableLT IProp := fun _ _ => inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ = _))

docString: `⊢ A`, causes failure during reconstruction

ref.pp:
/-- `⊢ A`, causes failure during reconstruction -/

  | sorry : Proof

/-- `(n: A) ⊢ A` -/

  | hyp (n : Name) : Proof

/-- `⊢ ⊤` -/

  | triv : Proof

/-- `(p: ⊥) ⊢ A` -/

  | exfalso' (p : Proof) : Proof

/-- `(p: (x: A) ⊢ B) ⊢ A → B` -/

  | intro (x : Name) (p : Proof) : Proof

docString:
* `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B) ⊢ A`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: A ↔ B) ⊢ A → B`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: A = B) ⊢ A → B`

ref.pp:
/-- * `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B) ⊢ A`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: A ↔ B) ⊢ A → B`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: A = B) ⊢ A → B`
  -/

  | andLeft (ak : AndKind) (p : Proof) : Proof

docString:
* `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B) ⊢ B`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: A ↔ B) ⊢ B → A`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: A = B) ⊢ B → A`

ref.pp:
/-- * `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B) ⊢ B`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: A ↔ B) ⊢ B → A`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: A = B) ⊢ B → A`
  -/

  | andRight (ak : AndKind) (p : Proof) : Proof

docString:
* `ak = .and`: `(p₁: A) (p₂: B) ⊢ A ∧ B`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p₁: A → B) (p₁: B → A) ⊢ A ↔ B`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p₁: A → B) (p₁: B → A) ⊢ A = B`

ref.pp:
/-- * `ak = .and`: `(p₁: A) (p₂: B) ⊢ A ∧ B`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p₁: A → B) (p₁: B → A) ⊢ A ↔ B`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p₁: A → B) (p₁: B → A) ⊢ A = B`
  -/

  | andIntro (ak : AndKind) (p₁ p₂ : Proof) : Proof

/-- * `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B → C) ⊢ A → B → C`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: (A ↔ B) → C) ⊢ (A → B) → (B → A) → C`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: (A = B) → C) ⊢ (A → B) → (B → A) → C`
  -/

  | curry (ak : AndKind) (p : Proof) : Proof

docString:
This is a partial application of curry.
  * `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B → C) (q : A) ⊢ B → C`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: (A ↔ B) → C) (q: A → B) ⊢ (B → A) → C`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: (A ↔ B) → C) (q: A → B) ⊢ (B → A) → C`

ref.pp:
/-- This is a partial application of curry.
  * `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B → C) (q : A) ⊢ B → C`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: (A ↔ B) → C) (q: A → B) ⊢ (B → A) → C`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: (A ↔ B) → C) (q: A → B) ⊢ (B → A) → C`
  -/

  | curry₂ (ak : AndKind) (p q : Proof) : Proof

/-- `(p: A → B) (q: A) ⊢ B` -/

  | app' : Proof → Proof → Proof

docString: `(p: A ∨ B → C) ⊢ A → C`

ref.pp:
/-- `(p: A ∨ B → C) ⊢ A → C` -/

  | orImpL (p : Proof) : Proof

docString: `(p: A ∨ B → C) ⊢ B → C`

ref.pp:
/-- `(p: A ∨ B → C) ⊢ B → C` -/

  | orImpR (p : Proof) : Proof

/-- `(p: A) ⊢ A ∨ B` -/

  | orInL (p : Proof) : Proof

/-- `(p: B) ⊢ A ∨ B` -/

  | orInR (p : Proof) : Proof

docString: `(p₁: A ∨ B) (p₂: (x: A) ⊢ C) (p₃: (x: B) ⊢ C) ⊢ C`

ref.pp:
/-- `(p₁: A ∨ B) (p₂: (x: A) ⊢ C) (p₃: (x: B) ⊢ C) ⊢ C` -/

  | orElim' (p₁ : Proof) (x : Name) (p₂ p₃ : Proof) : Proof

docString: `(p₁: Decidable A) (p₂: (x: A) ⊢ C) (p₃: (x: ¬ A) ⊢ C) ⊢ C`

ref.pp:
/-- `(p₁: Decidable A) (p₂: (x: A) ⊢ C) (p₃: (x: ¬ A) ⊢ C) ⊢ C` -/

  | decidableElim (classical : Bool) (p₁ x : Name) (p₂ p₃ : Proof) : Proof

signature.pp: (classical : Bool) (p₁ x : Name) (p₂ p₃ : Proof) : Proof

docString:
* `classical = false`: `(p: Decidable A) ⊢ A ∨ ¬A`
  * `classical = true`: `(p: Prop) ⊢ p ∨ ¬p`

ref.pp:
/-- * `classical = false`: `(p: Decidable A) ⊢ A ∨ ¬A`
  * `classical = true`: `(p: Prop) ⊢ p ∨ ¬p`
  -/

  | em (classical : Bool) (p : Name) : Proof

docString:
The variable `x` here names the variable that will be used in the elaborated proof.
  * `(p: ((x:A) → B) → C) ⊢ B → C`

ref.pp:
/-- The variable `x` here names the variable that will be used in the elaborated proof.
  * `(p: ((x:A) → B) → C) ⊢ B → C`
  -/

  | impImpSimp (x : Name) (p : Proof) : Proof

docString: A reified inductive proof type for intuitionistic propositional logic.

ref.pp:
/-- A reified inductive proof type for intuitionistic propositional logic. -/
inductive Proof/-- `⊢ A`, causes failure during reconstruction -/

  | sorry : Proof/-- `(n: A) ⊢ A` -/

  | hyp (n : Name) : Proof/-- `⊢ ⊤` -/

  | triv : Proof/-- `(p: ⊥) ⊢ A` -/

  | exfalso' (p : Proof) : Proof/-- `(p: (x: A) ⊢ B) ⊢ A → B` -/

  | intro (x : Name) (p : Proof) : Proof/-- * `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B) ⊢ A`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: A ↔ B) ⊢ A → B`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: A = B) ⊢ A → B`
  -/

  | andLeft (ak : AndKind) (p : Proof) : Proof/-- * `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B) ⊢ B`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: A ↔ B) ⊢ B → A`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: A = B) ⊢ B → A`
  -/

  | andRight (ak : AndKind) (p : Proof) : Proof/-- * `ak = .and`: `(p₁: A) (p₂: B) ⊢ A ∧ B`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p₁: A → B) (p₁: B → A) ⊢ A ↔ B`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p₁: A → B) (p₁: B → A) ⊢ A = B`
  -/

  | andIntro (ak : AndKind) (p₁ p₂ : Proof) : Proof/-- * `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B → C) ⊢ A → B → C`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: (A ↔ B) → C) ⊢ (A → B) → (B → A) → C`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: (A = B) → C) ⊢ (A → B) → (B → A) → C`
  -/

  | curry (ak : AndKind) (p : Proof) : Proof/-- This is a partial application of curry.
  * `ak = .and`: `(p: A ∧ B → C) (q : A) ⊢ B → C`
  * `ak = .iff`: `(p: (A ↔ B) → C) (q: A → B) ⊢ (B → A) → C`
  * `ak = .eq`: `(p: (A ↔ B) → C) (q: A → B) ⊢ (B → A) → C`
  -/

  | curry₂ (ak : AndKind) (p q : Proof) : Proof/-- `(p: A → B) (q: A) ⊢ B` -/

  | app' : Proof → Proof → Proof/-- `(p: A ∨ B → C) ⊢ A → C` -/

  | orImpL (p : Proof) : Proof/-- `(p: A ∨ B → C) ⊢ B → C` -/

  | orImpR (p : Proof) : Proof/-- `(p: A) ⊢ A ∨ B` -/

  | orInL (p : Proof) : Proof/-- `(p: B) ⊢ A ∨ B` -/

  | orInR (p : Proof) : Proof/-- `(p₁: A ∨ B) (p₂: (x: A) ⊢ C) (p₃: (x: B) ⊢ C) ⊢ C` -/

  | orElim' (p₁ : Proof) (x : Name) (p₂ p₃ : Proof) : Proof/--
`(p₁: Decidable A) (p₂: (x: A) ⊢ C) (p₃: (x: ¬ A) ⊢ C) ⊢ C` -/

  | decidableElim (classical : Bool) (p₁ x : Name) (p₂ p₃ : Proof) : Proof/--
* `classical = false`: `(p: Decidable A) ⊢ A ∨ ¬A`
  * `classical = true`: `(p: Prop) ⊢ p ∨ ¬p`
  -/

  | em (classical : Bool) (p : Name) : Proof/--
The variable `x` here names the variable that will be used in the elaborated proof.
  * `(p: ((x:A) → B) → C) ⊢ B → C`
  -/

  | impImpSimp (x : Name) (p : Proof) : Proof
  deriving Lean.ToExpr

instance instToExprProof : ToExpr✝ (@Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.Proof✝.{})
    where
  toExpr✝ := instToExprProof.toExpr
  toTypeExpr✝ := Expr.const✝ `Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.Proof []

instance : Inhabited Proof :=
  ⟨Proof.triv⟩

docString: Debugging printer for proof objects.

ref.pp:
/-- Debugging printer for proof objects. -/
def format : Proof → Std.Format
  | .sorry => "sorry"
  | .hyp i => Std.format i
  | .triv => "triv"
  | .exfalso' p => f! "(exfalso {p.format})"
  | .intro x p => f! "(fun {x } ↦ {p.format})"
  | .andLeft _ p => f! "{p.format} .1"
  | .andRight _ p => f! "{p.format} .2"
  | .andIntro _ p q => f! "⟨{p.format }, {q.format}⟩"
  | .curry _ p => f! "(curry {p.format})"
  | .curry₂ _ p q => f! "(curry {p.format } {q.format})"
  | .app' p q => f! "({p.format } {q.format})"
  | .orImpL p => f! "(orImpL {p.format})"
  | .orImpR p => f! "(orImpR {p.format})"
  | .orInL p => f! "(Or.inl {p.format})"
  | .orInR p => f! "(Or.inr {p.format})"
  | .orElim' p x q r => f! "({p.format }.elim (fun {x } ↦ {q.format }) (fun {x } ↦ {r.format})"
  | .em false p => f! "(Decidable.em {p})"
  | .em true p => f! "(Classical.em {p})"
  | .decidableElim _ p x q r => f! "({p }.elim (fun {x } ↦ {q.format }) (fun {x } ↦ {r.format})"
  | .impImpSimp _ p => f! "(impImpSimp {p.format})"

value.pp:
| .sorry => "sorry"
  | .hyp i => Std.format i
  | .triv => "triv"
  | .exfalso' p => f! "(exfalso {p.format})"
  | .intro x p => f! "(fun {x } ↦ {p.format})"
  | .andLeft _ p => f! "{p.format} .1"
  | .andRight _ p => f! "{p.format} .2"
  | .andIntro _ p q => f! "⟨{p.format }, {q.format}⟩"
  | .curry _ p => f! "(curry {p.format})"
  | .curry₂ _ p q => f! "(curry {p.format } {q.format})"
  | .app' p q => f! "({p.format } {q.format})"
  | .orImpL p => f! "(orImpL {p.format})"
  | .orImpR p => f! "(orImpR {p.format})"
  | .orInL p => f! "(Or.inl {p.format})"
  | .orInR p => f! "(Or.inr {p.format})"
  | .orElim' p x q r => f! "({p.format }.elim (fun {x } ↦ {q.format }) (fun {x } ↦ {r.format})"
  | .em false p => f! "(Decidable.em {p})"
  | .em true p => f! "(Classical.em {p})"
  | .decidableElim _ p x q r => f! "({p }.elim (fun {x } ↦ {q.format }) (fun {x } ↦ {r.format})"
  | .impImpSimp _ p => f! "(impImpSimp {p.format})"

ref.pp:
instance : Std.ToFormat Proof :=
  ⟨Proof.format⟩

docString: A variant on `Proof.exfalso'` that performs opportunistic simplification.

ref.pp:
/-- A variant on `Proof.exfalso'` that performs opportunistic simplification. -/
def exfalso : IProp → Proof → Proof
  | .false, p => p
  | _, p => .exfalso' p

docString: A variant on `Proof.orElim'` that performs opportunistic simplification.

ref.pp:
/-- A variant on `Proof.orElim'` that performs opportunistic simplification. -/
def orElim : Proof → Name → Proof → Proof → Proof
  | .em cl p, x, q, r => .decidableElim cl p x q r
  | p, x, q, r => .orElim' p x q r

value.pp:
| .em cl p, x, q, r => .decidableElim cl p x q r
  | p, x, q, r => .orElim' p x q r

docString:
A variant on `Proof.app'` that performs opportunistic simplification.
(This doesn't do full normalization because we don't want the proof size to blow up.)

ref.pp:
/-- A variant on `Proof.app'` that performs opportunistic simplification.
(This doesn't do full normalization because we don't want the proof size to blow up.) -/
def app : Proof → Proof → Proof
  | .curry ak p, q => .curry₂ ak p q
  | .curry₂ ak p q, r => p.app (q.andIntro ak r)
  | .orImpL p, q => p.app q.orInL
  | .orImpR p, q => p.app q.orInR
  | .impImpSimp x p, q => p.app (.intro x q)
  | p, q => p.app' q

value.pp:
| .curry ak p, q => .curry₂ ak p q
  | .curry₂ ak p q, r => p.app (q.andIntro ak r)
  | .orImpL p, q => p.app q.orInL
  | .orImpR p, q => p.app q.orInR
  | .impImpSimp x p, q => p.app (.intro x q)
  | p, q => p.app' q

docString: Get a new name in the pattern `h0, h1, h2, ...`
The context during proof search is a map from propositions to proof values.

ref.pp:
/-- The context during proof search is a map from propositions to proof values. -/
abbrev Context :=
  TreeMap IProp Proof IProp.cmp

docString: Debug printer for the context.

value.pp:
:=
  Γ.foldl (init := "") fun f P p => P.format ++ " := " ++ p.format ++ ",\n" ++ f

ref.pp:
instance : Std.ToFormat Context :=
  ⟨Context.format⟩

docString:
Insert a proposition and its proof into the context, as in `have : A := p`. This will eagerly
apply all level 1 rules on the spot, which are rules that don't split the goal and are validity
preserving: specifically, we drop `⊤` and `A → ⊤` hypotheses, close the goal if we find a `⊥`
hypothesis, split all conjunctions, and also simplify `⊥ → A` (drop), `⊤ → A` (simplify to `A`),
`A ∧ B → C` (curry to `A → B → C`) and `A ∨ B → C` (rewrite to `(A → C) ∧ (B → C)` and split).

ref.pp:
/-- Insert a proposition and its proof into the context, as in `have : A := p`. This will eagerly
apply all level 1 rules on the spot, which are rules that don't split the goal and are validity
preserving: specifically, we drop `⊤` and `A → ⊤` hypotheses, close the goal if we find a `⊥`
hypothesis, split all conjunctions, and also simplify `⊥ → A` (drop), `⊤ → A` (simplify to `A`),
`A ∧ B → C` (curry to `A → B → C`) and `A ∨ B → C` (rewrite to `(A → C) ∧ (B → C)` and split). -/
partial def add : IProp → Proof → Context → Except (IProp → Proof) Context
  | .true, _, Γ => pure Γ
  | .false, p, _ => throw fun A => .exfalso A p
  | .and' ak A B, p, Γ => do
    let (A, B) := ak.sides A B
    let Γ ← Γ.add A (p.andLeft ak)
    Γ.add B (p.andRight ak)
  | .imp .false _, _, Γ => pure Γ
  | .imp .true A, p, Γ => Γ.add A (p.app .triv)
  | .imp (.and' ak A B) C, p, Γ =>
    let (A, B) := ak.sides A B
    Γ.add (A.imp (B.imp C)) (p.curry ak)
  | .imp (.or A B) C, p, Γ => do
    let Γ ← Γ.add (A.imp C) p.orImpL
    Γ.add (B.imp C) p.orImpR
  | .imp _ .true, _, Γ => pure Γ
  | A, p, Γ => pure (Γ.insert A p)

signature.pp: : IProp → Proof → Context → Except (IProp → Proof) Context

type.pp: IProp → Proof → Context → Except (IProp → Proof) Context

value.pp:
| .true, _, Γ => pure Γ
  | .false, p, _ => throw fun A => .exfalso A p
  | .and' ak A B, p, Γ => do
    let (A, B) := ak.sides A B
    let Γ ← Γ.add A (p.andLeft ak)
    Γ.add B (p.andRight ak)
  | .imp .false _, _, Γ => pure Γ
  | .imp .true A, p, Γ => Γ.add A (p.app .triv)
  | .imp (.and' ak A B) C, p, Γ =>
    let (A, B) := ak.sides A B
    Γ.add (A.imp (B.imp C)) (p.curry ak)
  | .imp (.or A B) C, p, Γ => do
    let Γ ← Γ.add (A.imp C) p.orImpL
    Γ.add (B.imp C) p.orImpR
  | .imp _ .true, _, Γ => pure Γ
  | A, p, Γ => pure (Γ.insert A p)

docString:
Add `A` to the context `Γ` with proof `p`. This version of `Context.add` takes a continuation
and a target proposition `B`, so that in the case that `⊥` is found we can skip the continuation
and just prove `B` outright.

ref.pp:
/-- Add `A` to the context `Γ` with proof `p`. This version of `Context.add` takes a continuation
and a target proposition `B`, so that in the case that `⊥` is found we can skip the continuation
and just prove `B` outright. -/
@[inline]
def withAdd (Γ : Context) (A : IProp) (p : Proof) (B : IProp) (f : Context → IProp → StateM Nat (Bool × Proof)) :
    StateM Nat (Bool × Proof) :=
  match Γ.add A p with
  | .ok Γ_A => f Γ_A B
  | .error p => pure (true, p B)

signature.pp:
(Γ : Context) (A : IProp) (p : Proof) (B : IProp) (f : Context → IProp → StateM Nat (Bool × Proof)) :
  StateM Nat (Bool × Proof)

value.pp:
:=
  match Γ.add A p with
  | .ok Γ_A => f Γ_A B
  | .error p => pure (true, p B)

docString: Map a function over the proof (regardless of whether the proof is successful or not).

ref.pp:
/-- Map a function over the proof (regardless of whether the proof is successful or not). -/
def mapProof (f : Proof → Proof) : Bool × Proof → Bool × Proof
  | (b, p) => (b, f p)

signature.pp: (f : Proof → Proof) : Bool × Proof → Bool × Proof

docString: Convert a value-with-success to an optional value.

ref.pp:
/-- Convert a value-with-success to an optional value. -/
def isOk : (Bool × Proof) × Nat → Option (Proof × Nat)
  | ((false, _), _) => none
  | ((true, p), n) => some (p, n)

docString: Skip the continuation and return a failed proof if the Boolean is false.

ref.pp:
/-- Skip the continuation and return a failed proof if the Boolean is false. -/
def whenOk : Bool → IProp → StateM Nat (Bool × Proof) → StateM Nat (Bool × Proof)
  | false, _, _ => pure (false, .sorry)
  | true, _, f => f

type.pp: Bool → IProp → StateM Nat (Bool × Proof) → StateM Nat (Bool × Proof)

docString:
Reify an `Expr` into a `IProp`, allocating anything non-propositional as an atom in the
`AtomM` state.
Once we have a proof object, we have to apply it to the goal.

signature.pp: (g : MVarId) (Γ : NameMap Expr) (p : Proof) : MetaM Unit

docString:
A decision procedure for intuitionistic propositional logic.

* `useDec` will add `a ∨ ¬ a` to the context for every decidable atomic proposition `a`.
* `useClassical` will allow `a ∨ ¬ a` to be added even if the proposition is not decidable,
  using classical logic.
* `extraDec` will add `a ∨ ¬ a` to the context for specified (not necessarily atomic)
  propositions `a`.

ref.pp:
/-- A decision procedure for intuitionistic propositional logic.

* `useDec` will add `a ∨ ¬ a` to the context for every decidable atomic proposition `a`.
* `useClassical` will allow `a ∨ ¬ a` to be added even if the proposition is not decidable,
  using classical logic.
* `extraDec` will add `a ∨ ¬ a` to the context for specified (not necessarily atomic)
  propositions `a`.
-/
def itautoCore (g : MVarId) (useDec useClassical : Bool) (extraDec : Array Expr) : MetaM Unit := do
  AtomM.run (← getTransparency) do
      let mut hs := mkNameMap Expr
      let t ← g.getType
      let (g, t) ←
        if ← isProp t then 
          pure (g, ← reify t)
        else
          pure (← g.exfalso, .false)
      let mut Γ : Except (IProp → Proof) ITauto.Context := .ok TreeMap.empty
      let mut decs := TreeMap.empty
      for ldecl in ← getLCtx do
        if !ldecl.isImplementationDetail then 
          let e := ldecl.type
          if ← isProp e then 
            let A ← reify e
            let n := ldecl.fvarId.name
            hs := hs.insert n (Expr.fvar ldecl.fvarId)
            Γ := do
              (← Γ).add A (.hyp n)
          else
            if let .const ``Decidable _ := e.getAppFn then 
              let p : Q(Prop) := e.appArg!
              if useDec then 
                let A ← reify p
                decs := decs.insert A (false, Expr.fvar ldecl.fvarId)
      let addDec (force : Bool) (decs : TreeMap IProp (Bool × Expr) IProp.cmp) (e : Q(Prop)) := do
        let A ← reify e
        let dec_e := q(Decidable $e)
        let res ← trySynthInstance q(Decidable $e)
        if !(res matches .some _) && !useClassical then 
          if force then 
            _ ← synthInstance dec_e
          pure decs
        else
          pure
              (decs.insert A
                (match res with
                | .some e => (false, e)
                | _ => (true, e)))
      decs ← extraDec.foldlM (addDec true) decs
      if useDec then 
        let mut decided := TreeSet.empty (cmp := compare)
        if let .ok Γ' := Γ then 
          decided :=
            Γ'.foldl (init := decided) fun m p _ =>
              match p with
              | .var i => m.insert i
              | .not (.var i) => m.insert i
              | _ => m
        let ats := (← get).atoms
        for e in ats, i in [0:ats.size]do
          if !decided.contains i then 
            decs ← addDec false decs e
      for (A, cl, pf) in decs do
        let n ← mkFreshId
        hs := hs.insert n pf
        Γ := return (← Γ).insert (A.or A.not) (.em cl n)
      let p : Proof :=
        match Γ with
        | .ok Γ => (prove Γ t 0).1.2
        | .error p => p t
      applyProof g hs p

signature.pp: (g : MVarId) (useDec useClassical : Bool) (extraDec : Array Expr) : MetaM Unit

value.pp:
:= do
  AtomM.run (← getTransparency) do
      let mut hs := mkNameMap Expr
      let t ← g.getType
      let (g, t) ←
        if ← isProp t then 
          pure (g, ← reify t)
        else
          pure (← g.exfalso, .false)
      let mut Γ : Except (IProp → Proof) ITauto.Context := .ok TreeMap.empty
      let mut decs := TreeMap.empty
      for ldecl in ← getLCtx do
        if !ldecl.isImplementationDetail then 
          let e := ldecl.type
          if ← isProp e then 
            let A ← reify e
            let n := ldecl.fvarId.name
            hs := hs.insert n (Expr.fvar ldecl.fvarId)

          else

              let p : Q(Prop) := e.appArg!
              if useDec then 
                let A ← reify p

      let addDec (force : Bool) (decs : TreeMap IProp (Bool × Expr) IProp.cmp) (e : Q(Prop)) := do
        let A ← reify e
        let dec_e := q(Decidable $e)

      if useDec then

        if let .ok Γ' := Γ then

              match p with
              | .var i => m.insert i
              | .not (.var i) => m.insert i
              | _ => m

      let p : Proof :=

docString:
`itauto` solves the main goal when it is a tautology of intuitionistic propositional logic.
Unlike `grind` and `tauto!` this tactic never uses the law of excluded middle (without the `!`
option), and the proof search is tailored for this use case. `itauto` is complete for intuitionistic
propositional logic: it will solve any goal that is provable in this logic.

* `itauto [a, b]` will additionally attempt case analysis on `a` and `b` assuming that it can derive
  `Decidable a` and `Decidable b`.
* `itauto *` will case on all decidable propositions that it can find among the atomic propositions.
* `itauto!` will work as a classical SAT solver, but the algorithm is not very good in this
  situation.
* `itauto! *` will case on all propositional atoms. *Warning:* This can blow up the proof search, so
  it should be used sparingly.

Example:
```lean
example (p : Prop) : ¬ (p ↔ ¬ p) := by itauto
```

ref.pp:
/-- `itauto` solves the main goal when it is a tautology of intuitionistic propositional logic.
Unlike `grind` and `tauto!` this tactic never uses the law of excluded middle (without the `!`
option), and the proof search is tailored for this use case. `itauto` is complete for intuitionistic
propositional logic: it will solve any goal that is provable in this logic.

* `itauto [a, b]` will additionally attempt case analysis on `a` and `b` assuming that it can derive
  `Decidable a` and `Decidable b`.
* `itauto *` will case on all decidable propositions that it can find among the atomic propositions.
* `itauto!` will work as a classical SAT solver, but the algorithm is not very good in this
  situation.
* `itauto! *` will case on all propositional atoms. *Warning:* This can blow up the proof search, so
  it should be used sparingly.

Example:
```lean
example (p : Prop) : ¬ (p ↔ ¬ p) := by itauto
```
-/
@[tactic_parser 1000]
public meta def itauto : Lean.ParserDescr✝ :=
  ParserDescr.node✝ `Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.itauto 1022
    (ParserDescr.binary✝ `andthen
      (ParserDescr.binary✝ `andthen (ParserDescr.nonReservedSymbol✝ "itauto" false✝)
        (ParserDescr.unary✝ `optional (ParserDescr.symbol✝ "!")))
      (ParserDescr.unary✝ `optional
        (ParserDescr.binary✝ `orelse (ParserDescr.nodeWithAntiquot✝ " *" `token.« *» (ParserDescr.symbol✝ " *"))
          (ParserDescr.unary✝¹ `group
            (ParserDescr.binary✝ `andthen
              (ParserDescr.binary✝ `andthen (ParserDescr.symbol✝ " [")
                ((with_annotate_term"sepBy(" @ParserDescr.sepBy✝) (ParserDescr.cat✝ `term 0) ","
                  (ParserDescr.symbol✝ ", ") Bool.false✝))
              (ParserDescr.symbol✝ "]"))))))

@[tactic_alt itauto, tactic_parser 1000]
public meta def itauto! : Lean.ParserDescr✝ :=
  ParserDescr.node✝ `Mathlib.Tactic.ITauto.itauto! 1022
    (ParserDescr.binary✝ `andthen (ParserDescr.nonReservedSymbol✝ "itauto!" false✝)
      (ParserDescr.unary✝ `optional
        (ParserDescr.binary✝ `orelse (ParserDescr.nodeWithAntiquot✝ " *" `token.« *» (ParserDescr.symbol✝ " *"))
          (ParserDescr.unary✝¹ `group
            (ParserDescr.binary✝ `andthen
              (ParserDescr.binary✝ `andthen (ParserDescr.symbol✝ " [")
                ((with_annotate_term"sepBy(" @ParserDescr.sepBy✝) (ParserDescr.cat✝ `term 0) ","
                  (ParserDescr.symbol✝ ", ") Bool.false✝))
              (ParserDescr.symbol✝ "]"))))))

  | `(tactic| itauto!) => `(tactic| itauto !)
